preview_text:
The model favors Ivory Coast at 64%, with Curaçao offered just 12% to win outright. That 52-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial gap in the model's assessment of the teams' relative strength on a neutral ground.

The draw carries meaningful probability at 25%, suggesting the model sees a path to a stalemate. The win spread—Ivory Coast to 64%, Curaçao to 12%—indicates the match is expected to be competitive enough that a single goal could decide it, even if one side enters as heavy favorite. A draw remains plausible enough to shape tactical thinking.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books price Ivory Coast at 83%, nearly 20 percentage points higher than the model, while pricing Curaçao at 5% and the draw at 12%. Polymarket mirrors that tilt. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is more accurate; the divergence suggests meaningful disagreement between the statistical forecast and market pricing on the margin between favorite and upset scenarios.
